Lunch: Godt Brød coffee shop (skolebrød) or Mathallen Oslo food hall
Dinner: Den Glade Gris — Norwegian pork-focused gastropub (try the ribbe or nakkekoteletter). Or RIB for slow-cooked beef ribs. After dinner, walk to Fuglen — a vintage Norwegian café-bar for a nightcap
Activities: Fly into Oslo Airport (OSL): Depart from Pittsburgh (PIT) or Morgantown (MGW). EDT → CEST (+6h); Explore Aker Brygge waterfront: Stroll the harbor promenade with shops, restaurants, and marina views; National Museum: Munch's The Scream, Viking gold, and Norwegian art. Indoor, central, jet-lag-friendly; Walk the city center: Karl Johans gate, Royal Palace, Akershus Fortress; Oslo Opera House roof walk: Walk the sloping marble roof for iconic city + fjord views; Ekebergparken Sculpture Park: Modern art + forest trails with panoramic Oslo Fjord views. Light hike; Grünerløkka brewery crawl: Oslo Mikrobryggeri, Klosteret, Det Lille Bryggeri — craft beer for the adults
Group Notes: Arrive in Oslo. Keep Day 1 light — see Jet Lag Playbook. The National Museum (The Scream) and Opera House roof are must-dos. Activities during the day help power through jet lag.
Lodging: Budget: Citybox Oslo ($95/night) | Mid: Radisson RED Oslo ($118/night) | Premium: Grand Hotel Oslo ($285/night)

Directions: Take E6 north from Oslo through Gudbrandsdalen valley → Stop at Ringebu Stave Church (quick detour off E6) → Continue to Sjoa River for family rafting → Follow RV15 west to Lom
Lunch: Sjoa Rafting Center cafe — burgers, coffee, and Kvikk Lunsj for the road, or pack from Matkroken in Otta
Dinner: Fossheim Hotel restaurant — traditional Gudbrandsdalen lamb stew and local beer in a cozy 1830s timber building. After dinner, walk across the street to see Lom Stave Church lit up at night
Stops: Ringebu Stave Church: Built c.1220 in Gudbrandsdalen valley. Medieval interior with original furnishings; Sjoa River rafting: 11 km gentle rafting on Sjoa River. Guide, wetsuit, gear included. Class 1-2 rapids; Lom Stave Church: Built c.1158. One of Norway's largest stave churches. Open May–Sep
Activities: Ringebu Stave Church; Sjoa River rafting: Mild whitewater on Sjoa River. Guide, wetsuit included. Class 1-2 rapids — suitable for beginners; Lom Stave Church; Fossheim Bakeri: Norway's best bakery — famous kanelboller and bread; Lom village walk: Charming mountain village. Walk along the Bøvra River and explore the historic center; Medalen Disc Golf (18 holes): Flat course right in Lom village. Quick round for disc golf enthusiasts
Group Notes: Longest drive day but broken up with stops along the Gudbrandsdalen valley. Ringebu Stave Church is a quick detour off E6. The Sjoa River rafting center offers class 1-2 trips. Arrive Lom in the afternoon; Medalen Disc Golf is a free flat course right in the village. Fossheim Bakeri has Norway's best kanelboller — a great spot for pastries and coffee.
Lodging: Budget: Nordal Turistsenter ($90/night) | Mid: Fossheim Hotel ($140/night) | Premium: Røisheim Hotel ($250/night)
Points of Interest: Ringebu Stave Church; Sjoa River; Lom Stave Church

Directions: Take RV15 from Lom heading northwest → Turn onto RV63 at Grotli towards Geiranger → Stop at Dalsnibba Skywalk (toll road, 1500m) before descending → Descend Ørnevegen (Eagle Road) into Geiranger village — 11 hairpin turns
Lunch: Café onboard the fjord cruise — salmon wrap
Dinner: Brasserie Posten at Hotel Union — Norwegian seafood platter, reindeer fillet, cloudberries for dessert with panoramic Geirangerfjord views. After dinner, use the Hotel Union spa (pools, sauna) or sit on the terrace with a nightcap
Stops: Dalsnibba Skywalk: 1,500m viewpoint over Geirangerfjord. Europe's highest fjord view by road; Flydalsjuvet viewpoint: Iconic photo spot looking down Geirangerfjord. The classic postcard view
Activities: Dalsnibba Skywalk: 1,500m above the fjord. Unbelievable views over the entire Geirangerfjord; Flydalsjuvet photo stop: The classic Geirangerfjord postcard photo; Geirangerfjord cruise: UNESCO World Heritage fjord. The Seven Sisters and Friaren waterfalls. Lunch on board; Storseterfossen waterfall walk: Walk BEHIND a waterfall. Easy-moderate trail from Geiranger
Group Notes: Epic drive day — Dalsnibba and Flydalsjuvet are quick photo stops. The fjord cruise is the highlight. Evening: explore Geiranger village.
Lodging: Budget: Grande Fjord Hotel ($180/night) | Mid: Hotel Geiranger ($240/night) | Premium: Hotel Union Geiranger ($320/night)

Lunch: Westerås Farm café — local goat cheese waffles and berry jam with fjord views
Dinner: Naustkroa Café in the village — traditional Norwegian fish soup and local craft beer by the Geiranger waterfront. After dinner, walk down to the fjord shore for sunset photos
Activities: RIB fjord safari: High-speed RIB boat through Geirangerfjord. Get right next to the Seven Sisters waterfall; Geirangerfjord kayaking: Stillwater kayak on the fjord. Paddle right under the waterfalls. Suitable for all skill levels; Ørnevegen (Eagle Road) drive: Historic road with 11 hairpins and a panoramic fjord viewpoint at the top; Westerås Farm hike: Farm trail above Geiranger. Stunning fjord views from the meadow. Easy walk; Skageflå mountain farm hike: Abandoned mountain farm perched 250m above the fjord. Moderate fitness
Group Notes: Full day in Geiranger. Morning: RIB safari. Afternoon: choose between kayaking the fjord or hiking Skageflå. Evening: dinner followed by a casual Eagle Road sunset drive.
Lodging: Budget: Grande Fjord Hotel ($180/night) | Mid: Hotel Geiranger ($240/night) | Premium: Hotel Union Geiranger ($320/night)

Directions: Take Ørnevegen (RV63) up from Geiranger → Continue RV63 north through Valldal valley (famous for strawberries) → Cross Trollstigen mountain pass (check if open — late May borderline) → If Trollstigen closed: take E136 via Åndalsnes (adds 30 min) → Continue to Ålesund via E136 + E39
Lunch: Valldal strawberry farm café — fresh strawberry cake (late May)
Dinner: XL Diner — classic Norwegian seafood. Try the bacalao (dried salted cod stew — an Ålesund specialty since the Portuguese trade days), or the fresh catch of the day. After dinner, climb the 418 steps to Aksla viewpoint for the midnight panorama
Stops: Trollstigen Viewpoint: Dramatic viewing platform cantilevered over the mountain. 11 hairpin turns below; Gudbrandsjuvet Gorge: Spectacular river gorge with metal walkways over the churning water
Activities: Trollstigen mountain pass: Legendary mountain road with 11 hairpin turns. Cantilevered viewing platform. Iconic Norway; Gudbrandsjuvet gorge: Walkways suspended over a raging river gorge; Aksla viewpoint: Climb 418 steps to Ålesund's panoramic city viewpoint. Sweeping views over the archipelago; Art Nouveau architecture walk: Self-guided walk through Europe's most complete Art Nouveau city center
Group Notes: The Trollstigen drive is a bucket-list experience. Drive the pass at your own pace — cars can stagger by 10 minutes. If Trollstigen is closed due to snow, the E136 alternative through Åndalsnes is still scenic. Evening: walk up Aksla — 418 steps to the city viewpoint.
Lodging: Budget: Thon Hotel Ålesund ($140/night) | Mid: Quality Hotel Ålesund ($170/night) | Premium: Hotel Brosundet ($220/night)

Lunch: Café at Atlanterhavsparken — fish and chips with Atlantic views
Dinner: Apotekergata No. 5 — modern Norwegian shared plates in a historic Art Nouveau building. Great cocktails and local craft beer. After dinner, take a twilight stroll through the Art Nouveau streets to admire the spire-lit skyline
Activities: Atlanterhavsparken (Atlantic Sea Park): One of Scandinavia's largest aquariums. Huge ocean tank, penguins, seals; Kayak Brosundet canal: Paddle Ålesund's inner canals right through the city center. Stillwater; Sukkertoppen hike: Short but steep hike to a granite peak (314m). 360° views over Ålesund and the Atlantic; Alnes Lighthouse: Drive to Godøya island. Climb the lighthouse. Rugged Atlantic coast views; Molo Brew taproom: Harborside brewery in a historic pier building
Group Notes: Flex day in Ålesund. Morning: Atlantic Sea Park. Afternoon: choose between kayaking the canals or hiking Sukkertoppen. Late afternoon: Alnes Lighthouse. Evening: Molo Brew taproom.
Lodging: Budget: Thon Hotel Ålesund ($140/night) | Mid: Quality Hotel Ålesund ($170/night) | Premium: Hotel Brosundet ($220/night)

Directions: Take E136 east from Ålesund → Turn onto RV64 — drive the Atlantic Ocean Road → Cross 8 bridges between small islands — stop at viewpoints → Continue to the fishing village of Bud → Follow coastal road to Molde
Lunch: Bryggjen Restaurant in Bud — freshest fish soup and crab sandwiches
Dinner: Glass Restaurant at Scandic Seilet — floor-to-ceiling Moldefjord views. Try the bacalao or pan-seared halibut. After dinner, check the view from the Molde panorama terrace (222 peaks visible on clear evenings)
Stops: Atlantic Ocean Road viewpoints: 8 stunning bridges connecting islands over the open Atlantic. Norway's most scenic drive; Bud fishing village: Authentic Norwegian fishing village. Coastal fortifications from WWII
Activities: Atlantic Ocean Road: One of the world's most scenic drives. 8.3 km across islands with dramatic bridges; Bud fishing village: Charming coastal village. WWII coastal fort. Fresh fish right from the boats; Varden Molde Panorama: Famous 407m viewpoint over Molde, the Romsdal Alps, and 222 mountain peaks across the fjord
Group Notes: The Atlantic Road offers dramatic bridge crossings and photo opportunities at every stop. Stop at Eldhusøya rest area for the best views. Bud is a peaceful lunch stop. Arrive Molde late afternoon for the Varden viewpoint.
Lodging: Budget: Thon Hotel Moldefjord ($130/night) | Mid: Molde Fjordhotell ($150/night) | Premium: Scandic Seilet Hotel ($200/night)

Directions: Take E39 east from Molde along the Romsdalsfjord → Continue through the scenic Innerdalen valley → Take E6 south into Trondheim → Cross Trondheimsfjord (ferry or drive around)
Lunch: Coffee stop in Kyrksæterøra — traditional lefse and coffee
Dinner: Bakklandet Skydsstation — cozy historic inn on Bakklandet cobblestone street. Reindeer stew with lingonberry jam, fresh salmon, or fårikål when available. After dinner, walk across the Old Town Bridge for a Nidaros Cathedral night view
Stops: Kvernes Stave Church: Beautiful 14th-century stave church near Averøy. Quick detour
Activities: Kvernes Stave Church; Trondheimsfjord crossing: Drive around Norway's third-longest fjord; Nidaros Cathedral exterior: Scandinavia's largest medieval building. Evening exterior sighting; Evening walk Bakklandet: Trondheim's most charming neighborhood. Colorful wooden houses, cobblestone streets
Group Notes: Longest drive day but last big push. Break it up with Kvernes Stave Church and coffee stops. Arrive Trondheim late afternoon. Evening: walk through Bakklandet — Gamle Bybro (Old Town Bridge) is especially photogenic.
Lodging: Budget: City Living Schøller Hotel ($110/night) | Mid: Clarion Hotel Trondheim ($150/night) | Premium: Radisson Blu Royal Garden ($180/night)

Lunch: Kaffebrenneriet Bakklandet — best coffee and fresh pastries on the cobblestone street
Dinner: Farewell dinner at Credo (Michelin-starred new Nordic tasting menu) or Bula Neobistro (relaxed modern Norwegian, great wine list). End the trip at Trondheim Mikrobryggeri for a final Norwegian craft beer
Activities: Nidaros Cathedral interior tour: Crown jewels, rose window, crypt; Kristiansten Fortress: 17th-century fortress. Cannons, city views, Resistance Museum; Rockheim — National Museum of Pop Music: Interactive music museum. Hands-on exhibits, recording studios; Bakklandet & Gamle Bybro: Shopping and coffee in Trondheim's most photogenic neighborhood; Last-minute Norwegian souvenirs: Trondheim Torg and independent boutiques in Bakklandet
Group Notes: Full city day in Trondheim. Morning: Nidaros Cathedral. Mid-day: Rockheim museum — interactive music exhibits. Afternoon: choose between exploring Kristiansten Fortress or shopping in Bakklandet. Evening: farewell dinner.
Lodging: Budget: City Living Schøller Hotel ($110/night) | Mid: Clarion Hotel Trondheim ($150/night) | Premium: Radisson Blu Royal Garden ($180/night)

Lunch: Airport lounge or grab sandwiches for the plane
Activities: Fly home from Trondheim Airport (TRD): CEST → EDT (-6h). Connect via Amsterdam (AMS), Copenhagen (CPH), or Oslo (OSL). KLM, SAS, Norwegian. Return to PIT or MGW; Morning in Trondheim (if late flight): Last walk through Bakklandet, coffee at Kaffebrenneriet, or Ringve Music Museum; Duty-free at TRD: Aquavit, brunost, Norwegian wool
Group Notes: Departure day from Trondheim (TRD). Most US connections route through Amsterdam or Copenhagen. Plan flights to coordinate connections through AMS if possible.
